content:
# Revelation Chapter 10 — Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Book:** Revelation | **Chapter:** 10

**Verse 1 — "strong" vs. "mighty"; "feet" vs. "legs"; "clothed with" vs. "wrapped in":**
- EMET: "another strong angel coming down out of the heaven, having been clothed with a cloud...his feet as pillars of fire"
- LOGOS: "another mighty angel coming down out of heaven, clothed with a cloud...his feet like pillars of fire"
- PNEUMA: "another mighty angel coming down from heaven. He was wrapped in a cloud...his legs were like pillars of fire"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA adopts "mighty" (LOGOS/PNEUMA majority) for ischyron. "Clothed with" (LOGOS) preserves peribeblemenon faithfully. "Feet" retained over "legs" (PNEUMA) — podes means feet specifically.

**Verse 2 — "little scroll" phrasing; "placed" vs. "set" vs. "planted":**
- EMET: "a little scroll having been opened...he placed his right foot"
- LOGOS: "a little scroll that had been opened...he set his right foot"
- PNEUMA: "a small scroll that lay open...He planted his right foot"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS — "that had been opened" and "set" are faithful and natural. "Planted" (PNEUMA) adds connotations not in the Greek etheken.

**Verse 3 — "great voice" vs. "loud voice"; "spoke" vs. "uttered" vs. "responded":**
- EMET: "with a great voice...the seven thunders spoke"
- LOGOS: "with a loud voice...the seven thunders uttered their own voices"
- PNEUMA: "with a voice as powerful as a lion's roar...the seven thunders responded"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA adopts "loud voice" (LOGOS). "Uttered their own voices" (LOGOS) preferred — "responded" (PNEUMA) implies dialogic relationship not explicit in the Greek.

**Verse 6 — "time shall be no longer" vs. "delay no longer":**
- EMET: "time shall be no longer"
- LOGOS: "there shall be delay no longer"
- PNEUMA: "There will be no more delay!"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS — chronos here refers to an interval of waiting, not abstract time (EMET).

**Verse 7 — "is finished" vs. "is completed" vs. "will be fulfilled"; "mystery" vs. "hidden plan":**
- EMET: "the mystery of God is finished...proclaimed [the good news] to his own servants"
- LOGOS: "the mystery of God is completed...proclaimed the good news to his servants"
- PNEUMA: "God's hidden plan will be fulfilled...announced to his servants"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA adopts "is completed" (LOGOS) for etelesthē. "Mystery" retained over "hidden plan" (PNEUMA). "Proclaimed the good news" (EMET/LOGOS) preserved for euēngelisen.

**Verse 8 — "out of the heaven" vs. "from heaven"; scroll description:**
- EMET: "out of the heaven...the scroll which has been opened"
- LOGOS: "from heaven...the scroll which is opened"
- PNEUMA: "from heaven...the open scroll"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "From heaven" is natural English; "which is opened" preserves the perfect participle.

**Verse 9 — "saying to him" vs. "telling him" vs. "asked him"; "belly" vs. "stomach":**
- EMET: "saying to him...your belly bitter"
- LOGOS: "telling him...your stomach bitter"
- PNEUMA: "asked him...your stomach sour"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Stomach" preferred over "belly" for formal register. "Bitter" (EMET/LOGOS) over "sour" (PNEUMA) for pikranei.

**Verse 11 — Word order of "many"; "tongues" vs. "languages"; "concerning" vs. "about":**
- EMET: "concerning peoples and nations and tongues and many kings"
- LOGOS: "concerning many peoples and nations and tongues and kings"
- PNEUMA: "about many peoples, nations, languages, and kings"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Tongues" retained over "languages" (PNEUMA) for biblical register. "Concerning" preferred over "about" for formality.

---

*Signed, SOPHIA — in wisdom, seeking the faithful center where precision meets clarity*
